content.full_text:
MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER

MICHAEL R NAVE
STEVEN R. MEYERS
ELIZABETH H. SILVER
MICHAEL S. RIBACK
MICHAEL F. RODRIGUEZ
KATHLEEN FAUBION
FREDERICK S. ETHERIDGE
WENDY A. ROBERTS
DAVID W. SKINNER
STEVEN J. MATTAS

A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

PENINSULA OFFICE

1220 HOWARD AVE., SUITE 250
BURLINGAME CA 94010-4211
TELEPHONE: (415) 348-7130
FACSIMILE: (415) 342-0886

GATEWAY PLAZA
777 DAVIS STREET, SUITE 300
SAN LEANDRO, CALIFORNIA 94577
TELEPHONE: (510) 351-4300
FACSIMILE: (510) 351-4481

March 17, 1992

OF COUNSEL
ANDREA J. SALTZMAN

REPL TO:

San Leandro


Luisa Menchaca
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Request for Advice Regarding Council Members - Elect

Dear Ms. Menchaca::

     As we discussed in our telephone conversations on March 6
and 13, 1992, citizens of the area voted, in late 1991, to
incorporate as the Town of Windsor. However, because of LAFCO
regulations, the Town will not officially incorporate until July,
1992. In the election in which the voters approved the incorporation, they also elected Town council members. However, because
the Town of Windsor has not officially incorporated yet, the
council members are now Town council members - elect. They will
begin serving as Town council members when the Town incorporates
this summer.

     To assist in the organization of the Town and to ensure that
upon incorporation the Town is in a position to function, the
Council Members - elect have been meeting to discuss issues
concerning the future Town.

     Our questions concern the filing duties of the Council
Members - elect and Town officers (i.e., City Attorney,
Treasurer, et al.). Under Government Code § 87200, there is no
question that these officials are required to file economic
interest statements once the Town incorporates. However, the
immediate situation appears to be in somewhat of a gray area,
because the officials - elect are not yet officially council
members or officers, and because the Town is not yet incorporated. I
have reviewed the FPPC Opinion Index, and have not located any
opinions on this subject. In our telephone conversation on March
13, 1992, you informed me that you had reviewed the FPPC's letter
files, and had not located any letters on this issue.

Luisa Menchaca
March 17, 1992
Page 2


The Council Members - elect, along with the City Attorney
and other officials - elect, would like to meet their filing
obligations under the Political Reform Act. Our questions are as
follows: Are the Council Members - Elect, as well as other
positions listed in Government Code § 87200, now required to file
assuming office statements? Are they now required to file initial
economic interest statements? Or, should they simply wait until
the City incorporates in July, 1992 before filing any economic
interest statements? The Council Members - elect and Town
officers - elect will meet their filing duties, and are simply
requesting your advice as to what those duties are.

Any assistance you could provide on these questions would be
greatly appreciated. If you have any questions or require
further information, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Very truly yours,

MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER

Steven R. Meyers
Town Attorney
By: Fred S. Etheridge
Associate Attorney
FSE:kh

cc: Paul Williams, General Manager
Windsor Water District

However, because of LAFCO regulations, the Town will not officially incorporate until July, 1992. In the election in which the voters approved the incorporation, they also elected Town council members. However, because the Town of Windsor has not officially incorporated yet, the council members are now Town council members - elect. They will begin serving as Town council members when the Town incorporates this summer. To assist in the organization of the Town and to ensure that upon incorporation the Town is in a position to function, the Council Members - elect have been meeting to discuss issues concerning the future Town. Our questions concern the filing duties of the Council Members - elect and Town officers (i.e., City Attorney, Treasurer, et al.). Under Government Code § 87200, there is no question that these officials are required to file economic interest statements once the Town incorporates. However, the immediate situation appears to be in somewhat of a gray area, because the officials - elect are not yet officially council members or officers, because the Town is not yet incorporated. I have reviewed the FPPC Opinion Index, and have not located any opinions on this subject. In our telephone conversation on March 13, 1992, you informed me that you had reviewed the FPPC's letter files, and had not located any letters on this issue. The Council Members-elect, along with the City Attorney and other officials-elect, would like to meet their filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. Our questions are as follows: Are the Council Members-Elect, as well as other positions listed in Government Code § 87200, now required to file assuming office statements? Are they now required to file initial economic interest statements? Or, should they simply wait until the City incorporates in July, 1992 before filing any economic interest statements? The Council Members-elect and Town officers-elect will meet their filing duties, and are simply requesting your advice as to what those duties are. Any assistance you could provide on these questions would be greatly appreciated. If you have any questions or require further information, please do not hesitate to contact me. Very truly yours, MEYERS, NAVE, RIBACK & SILVER [Signature] Steven R. Meyers Town Attorney By: Fred S. Etheridge Associate Attorney FSE:kh cc: Paul Williams, General Manager Windsor Water District
